quick_facts.label: Role scope
quick_facts.value: A Veterinary Officer in a state animal-husbandry service delivers clinical veterinary care and typically supports public livestock-health functions such as contagious-disease vaccination, awareness work, breeding-service programmes and veterinary-hospital maintenance; exact duties vary by state and posting.

quick_facts.label: Core qualification
quick_facts.value: Recruitment commonly requires a B.V.Sc. & A.H. degree (or another recognized veterinary qualification). For example, the Meghalaya Public Service Commission required a B.V.Sc. & A.H. degree and a qualification included in the First or Second Schedule to the Indian Veterinary Council Act, 1984.

quick_facts.label: Professional registration
quick_facts.value: State-government recruitment notifications may require registration with a State Veterinary Council or the Veterinary Council of India (VCI). VCI direct-registration guidance asks applicants to provide a State Veterinary Council certificate along with a degree from a VCI-recognized university.

quick_facts.label: Hiring route
quick_facts.value: Entry is normally through the recruiting process specified by the relevant state government, public service commission or departmental recruitment body. Job titles are not uniform: comparable entry posts include Veterinary Officer, Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Officer, Veterinary Assistant Surgeon and Veterinary Surgeon Grade II.

quick_facts.label: Compensation context
quick_facts.value: Pay is state-, cadre- and notification-specific. As dated examples, Delhi’s 2024 Veterinary Officer recruitment rules specify Pay Level 10 (₹56,100–₹177,500) plus non-practising allowance, while a 2024 West Bengal appointment notification placed Veterinary Officers at state pay level 16, ₹56,100–₹144,300, plus admissible allowances.

sections.heading: Clinical care, livestock health and scheme delivery
paragraphs: The role combines professional clinical work with public-service delivery. A departmental statement of Veterinary Officer duties in Chandigarh includes veterinary treatment, mass vaccination against contagious diseases—including foot-and-mouth disease, haemorrhagic septicaemia and rabies—awareness and communication, frozen-semen work under cattle development, and maintenance of veterinary hospitals.

State officers may therefore contribute to programmes that go beyond individual case treatment: the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying describes the Livestock Health and Disease Control scheme as covering prophylactic vaccination, disease surveillance, capacity building and strengthening veterinary infrastructure. The local allocation of these functions depends on the state department, programme and posting.
bullets: Diagnose and treat animals presenting at government veterinary facilities or through assigned service arrangements.
Plan or participate in vaccination, surveillance and outbreak-control activities under livestock-health programmes.
Support livestock owners through awareness, communication and animal-husbandry extension.
Implement assigned breeding, cattle-development and other departmental schemes; maintain required facility and programme records.

sections.heading: How candidates typically enter
paragraphs: The immediate educational route is completion of B.V.Sc. & A.H. from a veterinary college whose qualification is recognized under the Indian Veterinary Council Act framework. VCI publishes the First Schedule of recognized veterinary qualifications, and its registration guidance refers to degrees from VCI-recognized universities.

Candidates then need to meet the precise eligibility in the applicable state notice. A 2025 Meghalaya PSC notification for Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Officer required B.V.Sc. & A.H., a recognized veterinary qualification under the Act’s schedules, and registration with the State Veterinary Council or VCI. Recruitment mechanisms, age limits, reservation rules, language requirements and examination/interview arrangements are notification-specific rather than nationally identical.

Applicants should monitor their state Public Service Commission, state animal-husbandry department and official recruitment portal under equivalent titles such as Veterinary Officer, Veterinary Assistant Surgeon or Veterinary Surgeon Grade II.
bullets: Earn B.V.Sc. & A.H. from an institution/qualification recognized in the Indian Veterinary Council Act framework.
Obtain and maintain the veterinary registration required by the recruiting state or union territory.
Apply to a state-specific official recruitment notification and complete its selection stages.
Check the notification for domicile, age, reservation, language, document-verification and category requirements.

sections.heading: Skills that fit the role
paragraphs: The work calls for sound veterinary clinical judgment together with population-health capability: vaccination delivery, recognition and reporting of disease risks, biosecurity-oriented practice and interpretation of animal-health needs at herd or community scale. These competencies align with the national Livestock Health and Disease Control programme’s emphasis on prophylactic vaccination, surveillance and veterinary infrastructure.

Because departmental duties also include farmer awareness, cattle-development work and maintenance of government veterinary facilities, effective officers need clear communication, field coordination, careful records and the ability to translate technical advice into practical guidance for livestock keepers.
bullets: Clinical examination, diagnosis, treatment and referral judgment.
Preventive medicine, vaccination logistics and disease-surveillance awareness.
Communication with livestock keepers and delivery of extension messages.
Documentation, programme reporting, facility management and coordination with field staff.

sections.heading: Work setting
paragraphs: Postings can combine government veterinary hospitals and field-facing animal-husbandry work. Chandigarh’s published duties explicitly include both veterinary treatment and veterinary-hospital maintenance, alongside vaccination campaigns, awareness activity and cattle-development work.

The service environment can also involve scheme-based and mobile delivery arrangements. Under the national Livestock Health and Disease Control framework, states may operate mobile veterinary units, with the veterinary doctor involved in deciding whether an emergent case is directed to the unit. The mix of clinic, farm visits, campaign work and administrative reporting differs by state and district.
bullets: Government veterinary hospitals, dispensaries or other departmental facilities.
Rural livestock communities, farms and vaccination or awareness camps.
District and block-level animal-husbandry programme administration.
Potential mobile veterinary-service deployments where the state operates them.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: Progression is governed by the relevant state service rules and can differ significantly between cadres. Official Maharashtra transfer listings show Veterinary Officer positions at both Group B and Group A designations and also list Assistant Commissioner, Animal Husbandry & Dairy Development, Group A; this illustrates that departmental structures may include movement into more senior veterinary or administrative responsibilities, but it does not establish a universal promotion ladder.

Professional development may also include postgraduate veterinary education. VCI’s draft postgraduate standards identify B.V.Sc. & A.H. or an equivalent qualification from a recognized veterinary college or university as the qualifying examination for M.V.Sc./Ph.D. pathways; candidates should verify the final applicable regulations and employer rules before relying on this route.
bullets: Advance under the applicable state cadre’s seniority, departmental-examination, vacancy and promotion rules.
Move toward supervisory, specialist, district-level or departmental administrative responsibilities where the state service structure provides for them.
Pursue M.V.Sc. or other authorized professional development where relevant to eligibility and service needs.

sections.heading: Pay and benefits: notification-specific context
paragraphs: There is no single all-India salary for this state-government role. Pay follows the employing state or union territory’s pay rules, the post’s group/cadre classification and the particular recruitment notification. Delhi’s Veterinary Officer recruitment rules, published in January 2024, list Level 10 in the pay matrix (₹56,100–₹177,500) plus non-practising allowance; this is an example for that employer, not a nationwide entitlement.

A separate West Bengal appointment notification dated February 19, 2024 states pay level 16 with a ₹56,100–₹144,300 matrix for Veterinary Officers, plus allowances admissible under then-existing state rules. Candidates should use the live vacancy notice and its linked service rules to assess current pay, allowances, probation and pension-related conditions.
bullets: Compare only official, dated vacancy notices for the recruiting state or union territory.
Distinguish basic pay matrix from non-practising allowance and other admissible allowances.
Confirm whether the opening is regular, contractual, deputation-based or project-based before comparing compensation.
